message:
Add HDD minimization harness, roaring bitmap renderer, unified evidence bridges, and focus indicator widget

- Add HDD (Hierarchical Delta Debugging) module to ftui-harness for automated test case
  minimization of widget trees that trigger rendering bugs — includes e2e tests for
  minimization convergence and widget tree structural reduction
- Implement roaring bitmap data structure in ftui-render for efficient dirty-region tracking
  during incremental screen updates, with proptest invariant verification
- Add evidence_bridges module to ftui-runtime bridging the conformance evidence system
  with runtime rendering decisions for audit trail support
- Add unified_evidence module consolidating multiple evidence sources (coverage, parity,
  performance) into a single queryable interface with e2e integration tests
- Expand focus manager with tab-order cycling, focus group boundaries, spatial navigation
  (arrow key directional focus movement), and focus-trap containers for modal dialogs
- Add focus indicator widget for customizable visual focus ring rendering
- Update module re-exports in harness, render, runtime, and widgets lib.rs files
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1csc.6","title":"Drag-and-Drop E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of drag-and-drop functionality.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Mouse Drag (dnd_mouse_e2e.rs)
- Drag initiation threshold
- Drag preview follows cursor
- Drop zone highlighting
- Drop acceptance/rejection

### 2. Keyboard Drag (dnd_keyboard_e2e.rs)
- Pick up with Space/Enter
- Navigate targets with arrows
- Drop with Space/Enter
- Cancel with Escape

### 3. Cross-Container (dnd_cross_e2e.rs)
- Drag between lists
- Type compatibility checking
- Drop position (before/after/inside)
- Drag into nested containers

### 4. Sortable Lists (dnd_sortable_e2e.rs)
- Reorder within list
- Visual placeholder
- Auto-scroll during drag
- Drag handle mode

### 5. Edge Cases (dnd_edge_e2e.rs)
- Drag cancel mid-flight
- Drop on disabled target
- Nested drop targets
- Long distance drag scroll

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Drag lifecycle (start, move, end, cancel)
- DEBUG: Hit testing calculations
- TRACE: Position updates per frame

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/drag_drop/

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Drag-and-Drop E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

-{"id":"bd-1csfm.4","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for HDD Test Minimization","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Hierarchical Delta Debugging (EV=18)

### Scope
Full integration test running proptest suite with HDD-enhanced shrinking. Injects known bugs into widget tree construction, verifies HDD produces minimal reproducing cases, and logs all reduction steps.

### Test Scenario
1. Define 5 known bugs in widget tree construction (off-by-one, missing style, wrong layout, null text, overflow).
2. Generate large widget trees (100+ nodes) via proptest that trigger each bug.
3. Run HDD shrinking: recursively remove subtrees while preserving failure.
4. Verify final minimized tree is genuinely minimal (removing any remaining node fixes the bug).
5. Compare HDD reduction ratio vs standard proptest shrinking.
6. Test on structured inputs: event sequences (50+ events), state configs, nested layouts.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each reduction step emits:
```json
{
  "event": "hdd_step",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "bug_id": "string",
  "step": u32,
  "level": u32,
  "input_size_before": u32,
  "input_size_after": u32,
  "nodes_removed": u32,
  "still_fails": bool,
  "elapsed_ms": u64
}
```
Final result:
```json
{
  "event": "hdd_result",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "bug_id": "string",
  "original_size": u32,
  "minimized_size": u32,
  "reduction_ratio": f64,
  "total_steps": u32,
  "total_time_ms": u64,
  "is_minimal": bool,
  "proptest_shrink_size": u32
}
```

### Success Criteria
- is_minimal == true for all 5 known bugs (no further reduction possible).
- reduction_ratio >= 80% (minimized is at most 20% of original size).
- HDD produces smaller results than standard proptest shrinking for structured inputs.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Total test runtime < 60s for all 5 bugs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:36.248650341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:01.204213336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1csfm.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1csfm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:36.248650341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1csfm.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1csfm.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:01.204160286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1d3z0","title":"epic(graveyard-beyond): SOS Barrier Certificates for Admissibility Guards","description":"Offline SDP (semidefinite program) solves for polynomial barrier certificate. Runtime evaluation: O(d^2), <30ns. EV=6.0 (Tier A). Apply to: frame budget admissibility, resize coalescing safety boundary, degradation level transitions. The barrier B(x) >= 0 inside safe set, B(x) < 0 outside. ARCHITECTURE: Python/Julia SDP solver (offline) produces const coefficient arrays → Rust evaluator (runtime) does Horner evaluation only. #![forbid(unsafe_code)]: runtime evaluator is pure safe Rust math. Fallback: hand-tuned thresholds. Repro: Python SDP script in tools/sos_barrier/ with requirements.txt.

**User Value:** Provides mathematically provable safety guarantees for adaptive controllers, preventing runaway behavior in edge cases.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Offline Python SDP generates barrier certificate coefficients
- Rust Horner evaluator checks certificates in <30ns
- Integration with BudgetController verified
- All existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:48:54.262387129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:15.387468831Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","graveyard","safety","statistics"]}
 {"id":"bd-1d3z0.1","title":"task(sos-barrier): Offline SDP solver for polynomial barrier certificates","description":"Pre-compute polynomial barrier certificates OFFLINE using Python/Julia SDP solver (SCS/MOSEK/Clarabel). Output: Rust const arrays of polynomial coefficients embedded in source code (no runtime SDP dependency). Process: (1) Define safe set for frame budget (2D: budget_remaining, change_rate) and degradation transitions, (2) Solve SDP in Python: find B(x) = sum c_ij * x1^i * x2^j s.t. B(x)>=0 in safe set, dB/dt<=0 on boundary, (3) Export coefficients as const COEFFS: [f64; N] in generated Rust file, (4) Include Python script in tools/ for reproducibility.  {"id":"bd-22q","title":"Implement Theme system with semantic color slots","description":"PHASE 4 - THEME SYSTEM

## Background
A Theme provides semantic color slots that map to actual colors. This enables consistent styling and easy theme switching (light/dark mode, custom themes).

## Technical Requirements

### Semantic Color Slots
pub struct Theme {
    // Primary UI
    pub primary: AdaptiveColor,
    pub secondary: AdaptiveColor,
    pub accent: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Backgrounds
    pub background: AdaptiveColor,
    pub surface: AdaptiveColor,
    pub overlay: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Text
    pub text: AdaptiveColor,
    pub text_muted: AdaptiveColor,
    pub text_subtle: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Semantic
    pub success: AdaptiveColor,
    pub warning: AdaptiveColor,
    pub error: AdaptiveColor,
    pub info: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Borders
    pub border: AdaptiveColor,
    pub border_focused: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Selection
    pub selection_bg: AdaptiveColor,
    pub selection_fg: AdaptiveColor,
    
    // Scrollbar
    pub scrollbar_track: AdaptiveColor,
    pub scrollbar_thumb: AdaptiveColor,
}

### Adaptive Colors
pub enum AdaptiveColor {
    Fixed(Color),
    Adaptive { light: Color, dark: Color },
}

impl AdaptiveColor {
    pub fn resolve(&self, is_dark: bool) -> Color {
        match self {
            Self::Fixed(c) => *c,
            Self::Adaptive { light, dark } => {
                if is_dark { *dark } else { *light }
            }
        }
    }
}

### Built-in Theme Presets
pub mod themes {
    pub fn default() -> Theme;      // Sensible defaults
    pub fn dark() -> Theme;         // Dark mode
    pub fn light() -> Theme;        // Light mode
    pub fn nord() -> Theme;         // Nord color scheme
    pub fn dracula() -> Theme;      // Dracula colors
    pub fn solarized_dark() -> Theme;
    pub fn solarized_light() -> Theme;
}

### Theme Detection
impl Theme {
    /// Detect dark mode from environment/terminal
    pub fn detect_dark_mode() -> bool {
        // Check COLORFGBG for light/dark background
        // Check OSC 11 background query (if supported)
        // Default: assume dark
    }
}

### Theme Resolution
Widgets reference theme colors:
  let style = Style::new().fg(theme.text).bg(theme.surface);

### Runtime Theme Switching
- Theme stored in Context or Model
- All widgets receive theme reference
- Switching triggers full redraw

## Tests Required
- [ ] All semantic slots have sensible defaults
- [ ] Adaptive color resolution correct
- [ ] Built-in presets complete
- [ ] Dark mode detection works
- [ ] Theme switching triggers redraw

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ semantic color slots
- [ ] Light/dark adaptive colors
- [ ] 5+ built-in presets
- [ ] Detection heuristics
- [ ] Theme switching API

 {"id":"bd-22wk8.4","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Roaring Bitmap Dirty Tracking","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Roaring Bitmaps (EV=18)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering with Roaring bitmap-based dirty region tracking. Validates that diff output is identical to BitVec baseline and logs dirty set metrics per frame.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize FrankenTUI with Roaring bitmap dirty tracking replacing BitVec.
2. Render all demo screens, performing incremental updates (text edits, scrolls, resizes).
3. On each frame, compute dirty regions using Roaring union/intersection.
4. Simultaneously compute dirty regions using BitVec (shadow comparison).
5. Verify diff output (escape sequences sent to terminal) is byte-identical.
6. Test edge cases: full-screen dirty, single-cell dirty, empty dirty, alternating rows.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each frame emits:
```json
{
  "event": "roaring_frame",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "screen": "string",
  "dirty_cells": u32,
  "dirty_rows": u32,
  "roaring_container_type": "array|bitmap|run",
  "set_size_bytes": u32,
  "union_time_ns": u64,
  "intersection_time_ns": u64,
  "diff_output_hash": "hex_string",
  "bitvec_diff_hash": "hex_string",
  "match": bool
}
```

### Success Criteria
- match == true for every frame (Roaring produces identical diff output to BitVec).
- Roaring set_size_bytes <= BitVec size for dense dirty patterns.
- Union/intersection times scale sublinearly with screen size.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Handles terminal sizes from 24x80 to 200x400.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:26.778108681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:00.118137315Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22wk8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-22wk8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:26.778108681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-22wk8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-22wk8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:00.118092672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-fbp","title":"Implement inline mode overlay redraw baseline","description":"PHASE 2.5 - INLINE MODE IMPLEMENTATION (CRITICAL)

## Background
Inline mode is a NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement. The overlay redraw baseline is the correctness foundation that works everywhere, before optimization.

## Technical Requirements (from Plan Section D)

### Overlay Redraw Strategy
1. Save cursor position (DECSC or manual tracking)
2. Move cursor to UI region start
3. Clear UI region only (never full screen!)
4. Render UI frame
5. Restore cursor position (DECRC)

### UI Region Anchoring
- Bottom-anchored (default): UI at bottom, logs scroll above
- Top-anchored (optional): UI at top, logs scroll below
- Configurable height: ui_height parameter

### Log Write Coordination
write_log() must:
1. Suspend UI temporarily
2. Print log line to scrollback region
3. Re-render UI in its anchored position
4. Maintain cursor contract

### Cursor Contract
After any present():
- Cursor is at the correct position per active policy
- UI region is intact
- Scrollback is preserved

### Never Clear Full Screen
The DECRC/ED2 pattern that clears full screen is FORBIDDEN in inline mode.
Only clear the bounded UI region using EL (erase line) or localized ED.

## Implementation

pub struct InlinePresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    anchor: Anchor,  // Bottom or Top
    cursor_saved: bool,
}

impl InlinePresenter {
    pub fn present(&mut self, buffer: &Buffer, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        // 1. Save cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b7")?;  // DECSC
        
        // 2. Move to UI region
        let (_, term_height) = writer.size();
        let ui_start = match self.anchor {
            Anchor::Bottom => term_height - self.ui_height,
            Anchor::Top => 0,
        };
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        
        // 3. Clear UI region only (line by line)
        for row in 0..self.ui_height {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[2K")?;  // EL - clear line
            if row < self.ui_height - 1 {
                writer.write_all(b"\n")?;
            }
        }
        
        // 4. Move back and render
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        // ... emit diff ...
        
        // 5. Restore cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b8")?;  // DECRC
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] UI renders at correct position (bottom/top)
- [ ] Scrollback is preserved after present()
- [ ] Cursor returns to correct position
- [ ] Log writes don't corrupt UI
- [ ] Resize re-anchors correctly
- [ ] PTY test: verify no scrollback corruption

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overlay redraw works on all terminals
- [ ] Cursor contract maintained
- [ ] UI region bounded, never full-screen clear
- [ ] Log coordination implemented
- [ ] PTY tests pass

## Performance Budget
- Overlay redraw: < 2ms (acceptable for correctness baseline)

 {"id":"bd-fp38v.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Evidence Ledger","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Evidence Ledger (EV=10)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering with evidence emission enabled across all 7 Bayesian decision points. Verifies schema compliance, no missing fields, and correct evidence structure for every decision.

### Test Scenario
1. Enable evidence ledger emission for all 7 decision points:
   a. Diff strategy selection (incremental vs full)
   b. Resize coalescing (coalesce vs immediate)
   c. Frame budget management (admit vs defer)
   d. Degradation level selection (normal/reduced/minimal)
   e. VOI (value of information) for measurement decisions
   f. Hint ranking for command palette
   g. Command palette scoring
2. Render demo screens that exercise each decision point.
3. For diff strategy: alternate between small edits (incremental) and full redraws.
4. For resize: send rapid resize events.
5. For frame budget: inject artificial load spikes.
6. For degradation: gradually increase frame times.
7. Collect all JSONL evidence entries and validate schema.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each decision point emits a unified evidence entry:
```json
{
  "event": "evidence_ledger",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "decision_point": "diff_strategy|resize_coalesce|frame_budget|degradation|voi|hint_rank|cmd_score",
  "log_posterior": f64,
  "top_evidence": [
    { "term": "string", "bayes_factor": f64, "direction": "for|against" }
  ],
  "action_chosen": "string",
  "loss_avoided": f64,
  "confidence_interval": { "lower": f64, "upper": f64, "level": f64 },
  "alternatives_considered": u32,
  "frame_id": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- All 7 decision points emit at least one evidence entry during the test run.
- Every evidence entry has all required fields (no nulls in required fields).
- top_evidence array has exactly 3 entries per decision (top-3 evidence terms).
- bayes_factor values are positive and finite (no NaN/Inf).
- confidence_interval.lower < confidence_interval.upper.
- Schema validates against JSON Schema definition.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is parseable and every line deserializes without error.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:34.466502948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T05:07:35.760241543Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-6i8zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:07.857688982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-fp38v.1","depends_on_id":"bd-fp38v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:34.466502948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-xn2","title":"Create CI/CD pipeline configuration","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - CI/CD PIPELINE

## Background
Continuous integration ensures code quality, test coverage, and performance budgets are maintained. Must run on every PR and push to main.

## Technical Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

#### Jobs Overview
| Job | Trigger | Purpose | Blocking |
|-----|---------|---------|----------|
| check | PR, push | Format, clippy, tests | Yes |
| coverage | PR, push | Coverage thresholds | Yes |
| benchmarks | push to main | Performance budgets | Warn |
| e2e | PR, push | End-to-end tests | Yes |
| docs | PR, push | Doc build verification | Yes |

#### Check Job
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --all-features
- cargo test --no-default-features

#### Coverage Job
- Use cargo-llvm-cov for coverage
- Thresholds:
  - Overall: ≥ 70%
  - ftui-core: ≥ 80%
  - ftui-render: ≥ 75%
- Upload to codecov.io

#### Benchmarks Job
- Run on push to main only
- Performance budgets from plan:
  - Cell comparison: < 1ns
  - Present (80×24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms
  - Diff scan: < 100µs
  - Layout solve (20 items): < 100µs
- Fail on 2x regression

#### E2E Job
- Run scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Upload artifacts on failure
- Test on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest

#### Docs Job
- cargo doc --all-features --no-deps
- Verify no broken links
- Upload docs artifact

### Matrix Testing
- OS: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- Rust: stable, nightly
- Features: default, all, minimal

### Caching
- Cache ~/.cargo/registry
- Cache ~/.cargo/git
- Cache target/ with hash key

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] check job with format/clippy/tests
- [ ] coverage job with thresholds
- [ ] benchmarks job with budgets
- [ ] e2e job with script execution
- [ ] docs job with verification
- [ ] Matrix: 3 OS × 2 toolchains
- [ ] Proper caching configuration
- [ ] Badge in README
